title: S. Venugopal Naidu vs Late Venkatalakshmamma on 15 June, 2015
body: S. RESPONDENTS (By SMT: APARNA REDDY, ADV. FOR V SRINIVAS FOR R1-6, NOTICE TO R7 TO R25 D/W) THESE WRIT PETITIONS FILED UNDER ARTICLE 227 OF CONSTITUTION OF INDIA PRAYING TO SET ASIDE THE ORDER DATED 18T DAY OF FEBRUARY 2015, PASSED BY THE IIND ADDL. FIRST CIVIL JUDGE AND JMFC, MYSORE DISMISSING I.A.NO.3 UNDER ORDER 1 RULE 10[2] R/W SECTION 151 OF CPC FOR IMPLEADING THE PETITIONERS 2 TO 4 AND ALLOW THE PETITIONERS 2 TO 4 TO COME ON RECORD IN THE FDP NO. 16/2013, VIDE ANN-A AND SET ASIDE THE ORDER DATED 4.3.2015, ALLOWING I.A. NO.5 AS PER ANN-B, UNDER ORDER 21 RULE 26 R/W 151 OF CPC SEEKING FOR THE 7 APPOINTMENT OF COURT COMMISSIONER, FILED BY THE R-1 TO 6 AND ETC. THESE WRIT PETITIONS COMING ON FOR PRELIMINARY HEARING B GROUP THIS DAY, THE COURT MADE THE FOLLOWING: ORDER The present petition is filed under Article 227 of the Constitution of India challenging the order passed under Order I Rule 10, C.P.C . in FDP.16/13. The petitioner had filed an application to implead him in the said final decree proceedings arising out of O. S.12 / 76 , a suit for partition and separate possession. 2. The said suit came to be decreed and final decree proceedings are in progress. During the pendency of the proceedings, an application under Order I Rule 10, C.P.C . came to be filed and the same was dismissed. As against the judgment and decree passed in O. S.12 / 76 , regular first appeal had been filed which was partly allowed, modifying the decree of the trial court. Against 8 such modification, R.S.A.411/86 was filed and it was dismissed on merits. 3. After the disposal of R.S.A.411/86, a review petition has been filed by this petitioner seeking review of the judgment passed in R.S.A.411/86, after a lapse of 17 years. During the pendency of the final decree proceedings, another application had been filed seeking staying of further proceedings in the final decree proceedings. The learned judge dismissed both the applications by the impugned order. 4. After going through the records, this court does not find any illegality or perversity in the impugned order so as to invoke Article 227 of the Constitution of India. Nothing comes in the way of the petitioner to pursue the review petition, and the final decree proceedings would be subject to the result or outcome 9 of the review petition. With this observation, the writ petition is disposed of. Sd/- JUDGE vgh*
